effects.name: Water reflection ripple
effects.technique: Flipped text clone (scaleY(-1)) is blurred with filter: blur() and faded by a gradient mask, then a horizontal shimmer is animated via @keyframes that skewX/translate or shift a repeating-gradient mask to fake rippling water on the reflection.
effects.markupNote: duplicated text element or data-text ::after for the reflected copy

effects.name: Hover spotlight reveal (radial mask)
effects.technique: Stack two copies of the text: a dim base layer and a bright top layer masked by a radial-gradient (opaque circle, transparent outside). On :hover the mask circle grows/positions to reveal the bright text only inside the spotlight.
effects.markupNote: two overlaid text copies (or text + data-text pseudo-element)

effects.name: Cursor flashlight follow
effects.technique: Same dim-base + bright-overlay layering, but the radial-gradient mask center is bound to --x/--y custom properties (mask: radial-gradient(circle at var(--x) var(--y))). A tiny mousemove listener sets the vars; the flashlight tracks the pointer over the text.
effects.markupNote: two text layers; minimal JS sets --x/--y vars
effects.browserSupport: all modern (minimal JS for var updates)

effects.name: Cursor gradient glow follow
effects.technique: A ::before or background radial-gradient(circle closest-side, color, transparent) positioned at var(--x) var(--y) sits behind/over the text, creating a soft colored glow halo that trails the cursor; vars updated via getBoundingClientRect + setProperty on mousemove.
effects.markupNote: single element (pseudo-element glow); minimal JS for vars

effects.name: Shine sweep shimmer
effects.technique: Set background: linear-gradient with a narrow bright highlight band over the base color, color: transparent + background-clip: text, background-size: 200%, then @keyframes animate background-position so the highlight glints across the letters.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Animated gradient flow text
effects.technique: Full multi-stop linear-gradient clipped to text (background-clip: text, transparent fill) with background-size oversized; @keyframes loops background-position for a continuous flowing rainbow/aurora across the glyphs.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Conic glow halo sweep
effects.technique: A conic-gradient highlight is clipped to text (or sits as a glowing pseudo-element behind it) and rotated by animating a @property-registered <angle> var so a bright lobe of light circles around the text; filter: drop-shadow adds the glow spill.
effects.markupNote: single element (pseudo-element for glow layer)

effects.name: Duotone split text (blend mode)
effects.technique: Two overlaid copies of the same text each tinted a different color, combined with mix-blend-mode (e.g. screen/lighten/multiply) so overlap regions blend into a third tone, producing a Spotify-style duotone glyph.
effects.markupNote: two overlaid text copies (data-text ::before/::after)

effects.name: Color-split half/half (hard-stop gradient)
effects.technique: A linear-gradient with two color stops at the same position (e.g. 90deg, c1 0 50%, c2 50% 100%) is clipped to the text via background-clip: text, splitting each glyph into two solid colors with a crisp seam; angle controls horizontal/vertical/diagonal split.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Diagonal slide color-split on hover
effects.technique: Same hard-stop gradient clipped to text, but background-size is enlarged and background-position transitions on :hover so the color boundary slides diagonally across the word, swapping the two halves.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Wipe reveal (animated gradient mask)
effects.technique: Apply mask-image: linear-gradient(to right, black, transparent) oversized via mask-size, then animate mask-position (or the stop %) so an alpha edge wipes the text into view; on :hover or @keyframes for a directional reveal.
effects.markupNote: single element (-webkit-mask for Safari)

effects.name: Peek slide reveal (clip-path)
effects.technique: A colored copy of the text sits behind/over the base via ::before with a clip-path inset; on :hover the clip-path inset transitions from fully clipped to 0, peeling the alternate-color text into view from one edge.
effects.markupNote: data-text attribute feeds a ::before/::after overlay copy

effects.name: Glow trail light streak
effects.technique: A bright clipped highlight band plus a layered, blurred copy (filter: blur + brightness) form a comet-like glow; @keyframes sweeps the band's background-position while text-shadow/drop-shadow trails the light across the glyphs.
effects.markupNote: single element (blurred glow via pseudo-element)

effects.name: Neon flicker glow
effects.technique: Layered text-shadow halos (several increasing-radius colored shadows) create a neon tube glow; @keyframes randomly drops opacity/shadow on a few frames to simulate an electric flicker.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: CRT scanlines overlay
effects.technique: A pseudo-element layered over the text uses a 0deg repeating-linear-gradient alternating transparent and a low-alpha dark stripe (e.g. transparent 0-2px, rgba(0,0,0,0.25) 2-4px) so faint horizontal raster lines sit on top of the glyphs. Often paired with pointer-events:none and low opacity.
effects.markupNote: Single element plus a ::before/::after overlay (or a sibling overlay div); no data-text needed

effects.name: CRT scan-sweep flicker
effects.technique: Builds on static scanlines by animating a full-screen overlay: a @keyframes loop nudges opacity/brightness of a faint rgba layer at high frequency (e.g. 0.15s) to mimic refresh flicker, and a second @keyframes translateY moves a soft scanline band slowly down the text for a rolling-bar effect.
effects.markupNote: Text element plus one or two absolutely-positioned overlay layers (flicker layer + moving band)

effects.name: Old-monitor curvature glow (vignette barrel)
effects.technique: Wraps the text in a screen container with large border-radius and an inset box-shadow plus a radial-gradient vignette overlay (dark at edges, transparent center) to fake CRT barrel curvature and corner falloff; a soft outer glow box-shadow simulates phosphor bloom. Pure curvature is faux; no true 3D distortion.
effects.markupNote: Text inside a screen wrapper element with a vignette overlay pseudo-element

effects.name: TV static / noise overlay (SVG feTurbulence)
effects.technique: An inline SVG filter generates Perlin noise via feTurbulence (type='fractalNoise', high baseFrequency ~0.7-0.9, numOctaves 2-3); the filtered rect is applied as a background or overlay layer over the text, with CSS contrast()/brightness() pushing it to harsh black/white static. Animating the feTurbulence seed (or randomizing baseFrequency) makes it crawl.
effects.markupNote: Inline SVG filter def + a noise overlay element layered over the text

effects.name: Film grain texture
effects.technique: Same SVG feTurbulence fractalNoise source as TV static but tuned for subtlety: lower opacity, soft-light or overlay mix-blend-mode, finer baseFrequency, and a gentle filter contrast() so the grain reads as photographic film texture over the text rather than harsh static. Can be static or slowly animated via seed.
effects.markupNote: Inline SVG filter def + low-opacity grain overlay element above the text

effects.name: Terminal / matrix green phosphor glow
effects.technique: Monospace text colored bright green (#0f0/#39ff14) with stacked text-shadow layers at increasing blur radii to simulate CRT phosphor bloom; a dark/black background and optional faint same-color box-shadow on the container complete the terminal look. Glyphs glow without any image assets.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Phosphor flicker (terminal power-on flicker)
effects.technique: Extends the green-glow look by animating the text-shadow glow and/or opacity with a @keyframes that drops the shadow to near-zero at a few irregular keyframe stops, producing a random on/off flutter like an unstable tube or neon sign warming up.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Hologram flicker (iridescent shimmer)
effects.technique: A multi-stop linear/conic gradient is clipped to the text with background-clip:text + transparent fill; a @keyframes animates background-position for a moving sheen and a filter:hue-rotate()/brightness() loop cycles the iridescent colors. Adding subtle opacity dips and a cyan text-shadow gives the unstable holographic flicker.
effects.markupNote: single element (data-text only if also adding ghost copies)

effects.name: RGB split glitch (pseudo-element clip)
effects.technique: Two pseudo-element copies via content:attr(data-text) are offset left/right and tinted (red text-shadow on one, cyan on the other); independent @keyframes animate clip-path:inset() to reveal random horizontal slices plus small translateX/skewX jitter, producing the classic torn chromatic-aberration glitch.
effects.markupNote: Single element with a data-text attribute feeding both pseudo-elements

effects.name: RGB split / chromatic aberration (SVG filter, single element)
effects.technique: An inline SVG filter duplicates the source with feOffset in opposite directions, isolates channels with feComponentTransfer (one layer red-only, the other green+blue=cyan), then recombines with feBlend mode='screen'. Applied via filter:url(#rgb-split) to one element with no data-text or pseudo-elements; the offset can be animated.
effects.markupNote: Single text element plus an inline SVG filter definition (no data-text, no duplicate text)
effects.browserSupport: SVG filter (inline SVG needed for Safari)

effects.name: Glitchy static text (displacement jitter)
effects.technique: An inline SVG filter combines feTurbulence noise with feDisplacementMap to warp the rendered text, while a @keyframes (or animated turbulence seed) rapidly changes the noise so glyph edges shiver and tear like a corrupted signal. Often layered with a faint static overlay for a broken-screen feel.
effects.markupNote: Text element plus inline SVG filter (turbulence + displacement)

effects.name: Blinking cursor terminal (typewriter)
effects.technique: Monospace text in a nowrap, overflow:hidden box gets a typing @keyframes animating width in ch units with a steps() timing function so characters reveal one at a time; a border-right (or ::after caret) blinks via a separate step-end @keyframes toggling its color/opacity at 50%.
effects.markupNote: single element (caret can be a border or ::after pseudo-element)

effects.name: Scan sweep (light bar across text)
effects.technique: A bright narrow linear-gradient band (transparent to white to transparent) is layered over the text and animated with @keyframes that translate or move background-position across the element, so a highlight bar sweeps the glyphs like a scanner pass. Can be masked to the text via background-clip:text for a glints-on-letters look.
effects.markupNote: Text element plus a gradient sweep overlay (or background-clip:text on a single element)

effects.name: RGB subpixel stripe text
effects.technique: A fine 90deg repeating-linear-gradient of red/green/blue stripes (a few px each) is clipped to the glyphs with background-clip:text and transparent fill, so the letters appear built from RGB subpixel columns like a magnified LCD/CRT panel. Optionally overlaid with a faint scanline gradient.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Dithered / ordered-dither text fill
effects.technique: The glyphs are filled with a tiled checkerboard/Bayer-style pattern made from layered conic or repeating gradients (or a tiny SVG noise) clipped via background-clip:text, simulating ordered dithering / 1-bit pixel shading. Combining two offset gradient layers with masks confines the dither to color-blend regions for a retro pixel-art fill.
effects.markupNote: single element (SVG pattern only if using noise-based dither)

effects.name: Breathing weight pulse
effects.technique: Loop a keyframe animation on font-variation-settings that eases the 'wght' axis between a low and high value (e.g. 'wght' 200 to 'wght' 800) with infinite alternate timing, so the whole word swells and thins like breathing.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (requires a variable font with a wght axis)

effects.name: Variable-weight wave across letters
effects.technique: Wrap each letter in a span carrying a --i index custom property, run the same wght breathing keyframe on every letter, and stagger them with animation-delay: calc(var(--i) * step) so the bold crest travels across the word as a wave.
effects.markupNote: one span per character, each with inline style --i (or generated by a splitter)

effects.name: Weight ripple from center
effects.technique: Same per-letter wght keyframe, but the delay is keyed to each letter's distance from the middle (calc(abs(var(--i) - var(--mid)) * step)) so the bold pulse radiates outward symmetrically from the word's center like a ripple.
effects.markupNote: one span per character with --i index; center index supplied or precomputed delays
effects.browserSupport: all modern (abs() needs recent browsers; otherwise precompute delays per span)

effects.name: Width morph (condensed to expanded)
effects.technique: Animate the 'wdth' axis in font-variation-settings (e.g. 'wdth' 75 to 'wdth' 125) on a loop or transition, stretching letterforms from condensed to expanded without changing weight.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (variable font with wdth axis)

effects.name: Slant / oblique sweep
effects.technique: Animate the 'slnt' axis (e.g. 'slnt' 0 to 'slnt' -15 degrees) to tilt letterforms into an oblique and back; unlike CSS skew this uses the true designed slant of the typeface, so strokes stay correctly shaped.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (variable font with slnt axis)

effects.name: Italic morph
effects.technique: Animate the 'ital' axis between 0 and 1 (or transition font-style normal to italic on a variable font) so upright letters smoothly reshape into their distinct italic glyph forms rather than just slanting.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (font must carry a true ital variation axis, e.g. Recursive)

effects.name: Optical-size showcase morph
effects.technique: Animate the 'opsz' axis across its range (e.g. 'opsz' 8 to 'opsz' 144) so the typeface restyles itself for display vs text sizes — thin hairlines and tight spacing at small opsz exaggerating into high-contrast display forms at large opsz.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (variable font with opsz axis)

effects.name: Animated grade (GRAD) pulse
effects.technique: Animate the custom 'GRAD' axis (UPPERCASE) up and down; grade changes apparent stroke density WITHOUT changing advance width, so the text darkens/lightens with zero layout shift and surrounding boxes never resize.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (font needs a GRAD axis, e.g. Roboto Flex / Recursive)

effects.name: Elastic weight on hover
effects.technique: On hover, transition the 'wght' axis to a heavy value using a springy/overshoot cubic-bezier timing (e.g. cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)) so the type snaps bold with an elastic bounce, easing back to normal on hover-out.
effects.markupNote: single element (per-letter spans optional for staggered elastic)

effects.name: Multi-axis style morph (weight + width breathing)
effects.technique: Animate two or more axes simultaneously in one keyframe set — e.g. 'wght' 100/'wdth' 85 morphing to 'wght' 700/'wdth' 100 — so the type continuously transforms between distinct designed styles (light-condensed to bold-expanded) rather than along a single axis.
effects.markupNote: single element
effects.browserSupport: all modern (font with the chosen axes)

effects.name: @property smooth-interpolated weight
effects.technique: Register a custom property with @property as <number> (initial-value, inherits), feed it into font-variation-settings: 'wght' var(--wght), then animate --wght; registration lets the browser interpolate the numeric value smoothly and reliably instead of jumping, even when transitioning a single axis through a variable.
effects.markupNote: single element (needs @property at-rule in stylesheet)

effects.name: Per-letter slant wave
effects.technique: Per-letter spans each animate the 'slnt' axis with a staggered animation-delay keyed off --i, sending a tilt that leans through the word letter by letter like wind passing across the text.
effects.markupNote: one span per character with --i index

effects.name: Weight + color morph wave
effects.technique: Per-letter spans run one keyframe that animates BOTH 'wght' and color (e.g. via an hsl hue) together, staggered by --i, so each letter simultaneously thickens and shifts hue as the crest sweeps across — a richer wave than weight alone.
effects.markupNote: one span per character with --i (and optional --hue)

effects.name: Variable display-font showcase loop
effects.technique: A self-cycling 'specimen' that walks the type through several named states in one keyframe timeline — pausing on light, bold, condensed, expanded, slanted — by stepping font-variation-settings across multiple axes at successive keyframe percentages, demoing the font's full range.
effects.markupNote: single element (optionally a label that swaps via content)

effects.name: Scroll/pointer-driven weight (no autoplay)
effects.technique: Drive the 'wght' (or any axis) from a CSS variable updated by scroll-timeline or a pointer-position custom property, so the type bolds/condenses in response to scroll progress or cursor proximity instead of on a fixed loop.
effects.markupNote: single element (scroll-timeline) — pointer variant needs a small JS/CSS var bridge for cursor
effects.browserSupport: needs scroll-driven animations / @property (recent Chromium, Safari, Firefox); pointer variant needs minimal helper

effects.name: Circular text on a path (SVG textPath)
effects.technique: SVG <text> with <textPath xlink:href> referencing a <path> arc/circle in <defs>; startOffset for positioning, text-anchor:middle for centering.
effects.markupNote: Requires SVG: <svg><defs><path id='curve' d='...'/></defs><text><textPath href='#curve'>TEXT</textPath></text></svg>. Cannot be done with a plain <h1>.
effects.browserSupport: All modern browsers (SVG textPath is widely supported).

effects.name: Text spinning around a circle (animated startOffset)
effects.technique: SVG textPath on a circular path with animated startOffset (CSS @keyframes on the textPath, or SMIL <animate>) to make the text rotate/scroll continuously around the ring.
effects.markupNote: SVG textPath markup required; animate startOffset 0%→100% (note: Firefox has historically not animated startOffset via CSS, fallback to SMIL or rotate the whole SVG).
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Safari full; Firefox may need SVG rotate fallback for startOffset animation.

effects.name: Wavy / sine-curve text following a path
effects.technique: SVG textPath bound to a smooth bezier S-curve so the baseline of the text undulates (banner / ribbon look), distinct from per-letter vertical jitter because glyphs also rotate to follow the tangent.
effects.markupNote: SVG path with C/S bezier commands; text rides the true curve so letters tilt along the slope.

effects.name: Background-inverting text (mix-blend-mode: difference)
effects.technique: White text with mix-blend-mode:difference (or exclusion) over a multi-colored / image / video background so each part of a glyph inverts whatever is behind it; pairs with a fixed background that scrolls under the text.
effects.markupNote: Single <h1> over a patterned/animated background element; set mix-blend-mode:difference on the text.
effects.browserSupport: All modern browsers (can cause scroll jank in Firefox).

effects.name: Hue / color blend text (mix-blend-mode: hue/color/luminosity)
effects.technique: Text layer using component blend modes (hue, saturation, color, luminosity) so the text borrows the hue of its own fill but the luminosity of the backdrop, producing color-bleed typography over imagery.
effects.markupNote: Plain text element layered over an image/gradient backdrop.

effects.name: Frosted glass / glassmorphism text
effects.technique: SVG <text> inside a <clipPath> used to clip an element with backdrop-filter:blur(); the glyph shapes reveal a blurred, semi-transparent view of the busy background behind them, plus a subtle highlight stroke for a glass edge.
effects.markupNote: Needs an SVG clipPath of the text applied to a div with backdrop-filter, sitting over a colorful/photo background.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Edge/Safari good; backdrop-filter needs -webkit- in Safari, limited older support.

effects.name: Soap-bubble / thin-film iridescent text
effects.technique: Layered conic + radial gradients animated with @property hue, combined with a soft inner/outer glow and slight blur to mimic the swirling thin-film interference of a soap bubble (distinct from flat holographic foil: emphasis on curved, blooming, pastel rainbow swirl).
effects.markupNote: Single text element; multiple stacked gradient layers via background or pseudo-elements.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Edge/Safari (needs @property for smooth animation; static fallback elsewhere).

effects.name: Matrix digital rain fill
effects.technique: Background-clip:text over an animated layer of falling green katakana/glyph columns (CSS gradient strips translated downward, or a tiled SVG/canvas pattern) so the text is filled with cascading Matrix rain rather than a flat phosphor glow.
effects.markupNote: Plain text with a tall animated gradient/pattern background clipped to the glyphs; optional pseudo-element column streaks.

effects.name: Scroll-driven gradient reveal
effects.technique: animation-timeline:scroll()/view() drives background-position or a clip/mask so the text fill, shimmer, or color sweeps as the user scrolls, with zero JavaScript.
effects.markupNote: Single text element; relies on scroll/view timeline so it only animates on scroll.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Edge full; Safari/Firefox partial (graceful static fallback).

effects.name: Scroll-driven variable-font axis morph
effects.technique: font-variation-settings (wght/wdth/slnt/opsz) animated via animation-timeline:scroll() so the headline thickens, expands, or slants in lockstep with scroll position.
effects.markupNote: Needs a variable font loaded; single text element driven by scroll timeline.

effects.name: Per-letter scroll-scrubbed reveal
effects.technique: Each letter wrapped in a span with a staggered view-timeline range so glyphs fade/slide/rotate in one-by-one tied to scroll, producing scrubbable kinetic typography without JS.
effects.markupNote: Requires splitting text into per-letter spans (build-time or JS) but the animation itself is pure CSS scroll timeline.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Edge; fallback to visible text elsewhere.

effects.name: Repeated echo / motion-trail text (stacked clones)
effects.technique: Multiple absolutely-stacked copies of the same text (real DOM clones or layered text-shadow/pseudo-elements) offset and fading in opacity to create a ghosting motion-trail / strobe afterimage look.
effects.markupNote: Best with a few duplicated text nodes (data-text clones) so each trail copy can have its own offset/opacity.

effects.name: Halftone dot-matrix animated fill
effects.technique: radial-gradient dot pattern (background-clip:text) whose dot size/position animates, or an SVG feImage + halftone screen, so the text reads as a moving comic/print halftone rather than a static dot fill.
effects.markupNote: Single text element; animate background-position/size of the dot pattern.

effects.name: Confetti / particle burst text
effects.technique: Many small pseudo-element or box-shadow particles spawned around/within the text that scatter outward with transform + opacity keyframes on load or hover (celebratory pop), text remaining as the anchor.
effects.markupNote: Particles can be generated with stacked box-shadows or a handful of pseudo-elements; pure CSS but verbose.

effects.name: Caustics / underwater light text
effects.technique: SVG feTurbulence + feColorMatrix generating animated caustic light ripples used as a clipped fill or overlay over the text, simulating dappled light at the bottom of a pool (distinct from water-fill or refraction: focus on moving bright caustic webbing).
effects.markupNote: Needs an SVG filter def for the animated turbulence; applied as fill/overlay on the text.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Safari good; Firefox SVG-filter animation may differ.

effects.name: Liquid glass lens / refraction text
effects.technique: Apple-style 'liquid glass': backdrop-filter blur + saturate combined with an SVG feDisplacementMap to bend the background through the glyph edges, plus specular highlight and chromatic fringe for a refractive lens look.
effects.markupNote: Requires SVG displacement filter + a clipped backdrop-filter layer over a busy background.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Edge best; Safari partial; Firefox limited (feDisplacementMap on backdrop unsupported).

effects.name: Glitch datamosh slice-scroll (RGB layer scroll)
effects.technique: Three color-channel clones (data-text) with mix-blend-mode:screen, each independently sliced via clip-path and translated on a fast jitter loop, producing a continuous scrolling/tearing chromatic glitch rather than a one-shot entrance.
effects.markupNote: Uses data-text attribute + ::before/::after channel clones; pure CSS.

effects.name: Distress / grunge stamped text (mask texture)
effects.technique: A noise/grunge texture (SVG feTurbulence or PNG) used as a mask-image so chunks of the solid-colored glyphs are eaten away, giving a worn rubber-stamp / letterpress-distress look.
effects.markupNote: Single text element; mask-image set to a generated/embedded grunge texture.

effects.name: Torn / ripped paper split text
effects.technique: Two clipped copies of the text split along a jagged clip-path edge, offset vertically with a thin shadow at the tear line, mimicking ripped paper or a torn poster; can animate the halves sliding apart.
effects.markupNote: data-text duplicate with two clip-paths; pure CSS, optional hover to separate.

effects.name: Embossed metallic plate (metal + bevel + specular)
effects.technique: Combines a metallic linear-gradient fill with an SVG feSpecularLighting/feGaussianBlur bevel filter and inset/outset shadows to read as letters stamped into a polished metal plate (distinct from flat chrome gradient and from generic emboss: metal sheen + raised relief together).
effects.markupNote: Needs SVG lighting filter for the bevel; combined with gradient fill on the text.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Safari good; Firefox SVG lighting differs slightly.

effects.name: Neon sign self-drawing tube (animated stroke-dashoffset)
effects.technique: SVG text-as-path (or outlined font) with stroke-dasharray/stroke-dashoffset animated to 'draw' the neon tube, then a glow filter switches on at the end to light the sign (combines draw-on with neon ignition, distinct from plain draw-on stroke).
effects.markupNote: Requires SVG path outlines of the text (converted glyphs) so stroke-dashoffset can animate.

effects.name: Rough.js-style hand-sketched outline text
effects.technique: Irregular, slightly-wobbly hand-drawn SVG stroke outlines of the glyphs (double-pass sketchy strokes) optionally animated with stroke-dashoffset for a doodle/marker hand-lettering effect.
effects.markupNote: Needs SVG glyph paths; sketch wobble via small feTurbulence displacement or duplicated jittered strokes.

effects.name: ASCII / dot-LED matrix display text
effects.technique: A repeating radial-gradient dot grid masked by the glyph shapes (or per-cell box-shadow grid) so the text looks rendered on a dot-matrix LED / scoreboard panel; can animate a scrolling marquee or flicker per dot.
effects.markupNote: Single text element with a dot-grid pattern clipped/masked to the glyphs.

effects.name: Split-flap / departure board flip text
effects.technique: Each character on a split-flap card that rotateX-flips through intermediate glyphs to settle on the final letter, mimicking airport/train mechanical flap boards (distinct from simple 3D card flip: continuous multi-flap cycling to a target).
effects.markupNote: Needs per-letter card markup (top/bottom halves) to sell the mechanical flap; CSS-driven.

effects.name: Odometer / slot-machine rolling digits
effects.technique: Vertical strips of digits 0-9 inside an overflow-hidden mask; translateY rolls each column to land on the target number, like an odometer or slot machine counter.
effects.markupNote: Requires a column of digit glyphs per character position; CSS handles the roll.

effects.name: Liquid drip + stretch goo (SVG gooey filter)
effects.technique: SVG gooey filter (feGaussianBlur + feColorMatrix alpha-contrast) applied to the text plus descending pseudo-element 'drops' so the bottoms of letters bulge and pinch off into falling blobs (true metaball goo, distinct from clip-path drip and gooey blob morph).
effects.markupNote: Needs the gooey SVG filter def and drip pseudo-elements/spans under the text.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Safari good; Firefox filter rendering varies.

effects.name: Icy frost-creep / freeze-over fill
effects.technique: Animated mask/gradient that sweeps from edges inward turning warm text into frosted blue-white with a crystalline noise overlay, simulating ice creeping across the glyphs over time (distinct from static crystalline ice and moving shine: a directional freeze transition).
effects.markupNote: Single text element; animate a mask wipe plus a frost-texture overlay.

effects.name: Dissolve / disintegrate into particles
effects.technique: Thanos-style disintegration using an SVG feTurbulence + feDisplacementMap whose scale animates up while opacity/mask erodes, scattering the glyphs into drifting dust particles (distinct from smoke drift and pixel smear: granular particle dispersal).
effects.markupNote: Needs SVG displacement filter with animated scale; applied to the text layer.
effects.browserSupport: Chrome/Safari good; Firefox displacement animation limited.

effects.name: Liquid mercury / metaball merge text
effects.technique: Gooey SVG threshold filter over reflective metallic-gradient glyphs so adjacent letters blob and merge like liquid mercury when they move/scale, with specular highlights riding the surface.
effects.markupNote: Requires gooey filter def + animated per-letter spacing/scale to trigger the merges.

effects.name: Audio / waveform reactive bar fill
effects.technique: Text fill driven by an animated equalizer of clipped vertical bars (CSS bars or conic/linear gradient stripes whose heights pulse on a loop) so the letters look like they're bouncing to music; visual-only loop, no audio API required.
effects.markupNote: Single text element; an animated bar pattern is clipped to the glyphs (CSS custom properties stagger bar heights).

effects.name: Liquid wave fill with surface foam (animated SVG wave)
effects.technique: An animated SVG sine-wave path that rises as a fill level behind the glyphs (clip/mask to text) with a translucent foam crest and bobbing motion, distinct from the listed rising-wave by emphasizing a real animated wave path + crest highlight.
effects.markupNote: SVG wave path animated horizontally + raised; clipped to the text shape.

effects.name: Paper cut-out layered shadow (paper craft)
effects.technique: Several stacked offset copies of the text in graduated paper-tone colors with soft drop-shadows between layers, producing a hand-cut layered paper / kirigami depth look (distinct from hard retro extrude: soft shadows + pastel layered tones).
effects.markupNote: Can be done with a long layered text-shadow stack on one element.

effects.name: Tie-dye / fluid marble swirl fill
effects.technique: Multiple overlapping conic + radial gradients (or SVG turbulence color map) clipped to text creating an organic swirling tie-dye / paint-marble pattern, optionally slowly drifting (distinct from marble/granite stone: vivid multicolor fluid swirl).
effects.markupNote: Single text element; stacked gradient layers clipped to glyphs.

effects.name: Cursor-repel / scatter letters on hover
effects.technique: Per-letter spans that translate away from the pointer position (CSS using pointer-tracking custom properties or :hover per-letter) so letters flee/scatter from the cursor and ease back, a playful interactive kinetic effect.
effects.markupNote: Needs per-letter spans; minimal JS (or CSS :hover approximation) to feed pointer position to letters.
effects.browserSupport: All modern browsers (full effect benefits from pointer JS hook).

effects.name: Magnetic / gravity attract letters on hover
effects.technique: Inverse of repel: per-letter spans lean toward the cursor and scale slightly, giving a magnetic pull / clustering feel, springy return on leave.
effects.markupNote: Per-letter spans + pointer position; CSS transforms.

effects.name: Hover ripple / wave-through letters
effects.technique: Hovering anywhere over the word triggers a ripple that propagates letter-to-letter (staggered transition-delay based on index distance), a Mexican-wave bounce/lift across the text on interaction rather than autoplay.
effects.markupNote: Per-letter spans with index-based --i custom property driving delays.

effects.name: Scroll-scrubbed 3D flip
effects.technique: Wrap the word (or each letter) in a perspective container and define @keyframes that rotate rotateX(-82deg) -> 0deg -> rotateX(82deg); bind them with animation-timeline: view(block) and animation-range cover 0%..100% so the slab tips up through upright exactly at mid-viewport and tips away past it. Scrolling scrubs the tilt both ways - the reader drives the rotation, no time loop.
effects.markupNote: single element (perspective wrapper); optional one span per letter for a staggered flip

effects.name: Scroll parallax depth stack
effects.technique: Emit several stacked data-text clones of the glyphs in graduated shades behind the face. Give each clone its own @keyframes translateY and bind them to animation-timeline: scroll(root block) so deeper clones travel further per unit of scroll than shallow ones. The extrusion visibly opens and closes as the page scrolls - 3D depth that lives on the scrollbar rather than the cursor.
effects.markupNote: data-text attribute (multiple ::before/::after + attr() copies)

effects.name: Scroll progress fill rise
effects.technique: Render hollow stroked glyphs (transparent fill + -webkit-text-stroke) over a background-clip:text colored layer masked by a bottom-up gradient/inset clip. Animate the fill level 0%..100% with animation-timeline: scroll(root block) so the color rises through the letters exactly in step with page-scroll progress - the headline doubles as a reading-progress meter.
effects.markupNote: single element (fill layer behind a stroked copy)

effects.name: Scroll glitch intensity
effects.technique: Two data-text pseudo copies (cyan/magenta) whose translate offset and clip-path slice amplitude scale from 0 to max via @keyframes bound to animation-timeline: view(block). The text is clean and legible when the heading is centred in the viewport and shreds into RGB chromatic tears as it approaches the top/bottom edges - glitch magnitude mapped to scroll.
effects.markupNote: data-text attribute (attr() pseudo copies)

effects.name: Scroll tracking spread
effects.technique: Bind @keyframes that animate letter-spacing from tight to wide (with a slight opacity/blur ease) to animation-timeline: view(block) cover 0%..100%. As the headline scrolls through the viewport its tracking breathes wide open then draws back shut - a scroll-scrubbed kinetic spread on a single element, no per-letter markup required.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Scroll neon charge-up
effects.technique: Layered text-shadow glow whose blur radii and a hot-white core (a second stroked layer's opacity) ramp from an unlit, near-dead tube to a fully-lit buzzing sign via @keyframes bound to animation-timeline: scroll(root block). The neon powers on as the reader scrolls the page in and dims back out on scroll-up.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Woven knockout mesh
effects.technique: Fill the glyphs with background-clip:text (solid or gradient), then apply two -webkit-mask layers - a horizontal and a vertical repeating-linear-gradient of hard opaque/transparent bands - combined with mask-composite: intersect. The intersection punches a perforated mesh of transparent slots out of the letters so the page background shows through a woven grid. Optional slow mask-position drift makes the weave crawl.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Swoosh-in motion-path entrance
effects.technique: Give each letter span offset-path: path('<a curved bezier swoosh>') and offset-rotate: auto, then animate offset-distance 100% -> 0 with a fade, staggered per index. Letters fly in banking along the curve and settle at 0% - a curved flight trajectory instead of a straight drop.
effects.markupNote: one span per letter

effects.name: Motion-path conveyor orbit
effects.technique: All letter spans share one looping offset-path (a horizontal sine ribbon or figure-8), each parked at a staggered offset-distance and animating +100% continuously with offset-rotate: auto so the glyphs bank as they round the curve. Letters flow endlessly around the ribbon like a conveyor belt - arbitrary path shape, no SVG textPath.
effects.markupNote: one span per letter

effects.name: Die-cut corner chips
effects.technique: Set each letter (or the whole word) on a solid background chip and shape the chip with corner-shape: notch / scoop / bevel alongside border-radius, carving die-cut ticket-stub, scalloped, or chamfered corners that border-radius alone cannot make. Because unknown corner-shape parses away, it degrades cleanly to plain rounded chips off Chromium.
effects.markupNote: one span per letter (chip per glyph) | or single element chip

effects.name: Bokeh light fill
effects.technique: Fill the glyphs via background-clip:text with a stack of large, soft radial-gradients (each a blurry colored disc at low alpha) over a dark base, so the letters read as out-of-focus lens bokeh. Drift the discs with an animated background-position and pulse a couple of them via opacity keyframes for a gentle twinkle. On light themes swap to warm defocused orbs on a tinted base.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Clean sliced type
effects.technique: Layer a data-text pseudo-copy exactly over the word; clip the base to the top half and the copy to the bottom half with two complementary clip-path polygons at a tunable slice angle. On entrance (and hover replay) shear the halves apart along the cut axis by a few pixels, hold, and settle back, leaving a crisp diagonal gap line. No RGB channels, no jitter - a designed ninja cut.
effects.markupNote: data-text attribute copy

effects.name: Shatter shards entrance
effects.technique: Stack 6-8 absolutely-positioned copies of the word, each clip-path'd to a different triangular polygon shard that together tile the whole glyph run. On entrance every shard flies out and spins in 3D (translate + rotate3d on a shared perspective, staggered) then snaps back into perfect registration, so the headline visibly shatters and reassembles. Replays on hover.
effects.markupNote: 6-8 stacked word copies, each clip-path'd to a shard

effects.name: CRT power collapse
effects.technique: Emulate an old tube switching on: keyframe the word from a single bright horizontal line (scaleY~0 + high brightness filter) that flashes, then expands vertically into full type, with a fast scaleX overshoot; a thin bright pseudo-element bar flashes at the collapse line. Run it as a one-shot power-on entrance so the resting state stays fully legible; replay on hover.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Sparkle glints overlay
effects.technique: Overlay a handful of four-point star glints on the glyphs, each built from crossed linear-gradient rays plus a radial core, placed via multiple background layers or salted pseudo/box-shadow points. Animate each glint's scale and opacity on staggered delays so sparkles wink on and off at different spots, like diamond twinkle, while the text stays fully readable underneath.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Prismatic dispersion fringe
effects.technique: Build a continuous rainbow edge dispersion from many hue-stepped text-shadow layers, each offset a hair further along one axis (red->orange->...->violet), so the glyph edges bleed into a full-spectrum smear like light through a prism. A slow shimmer breathes the offset amount, sliding the spectrum in and out. Single element, no clones - purely layered shadows.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Radar sweep reveal
effects.technique: Set the word dim, then reveal/brighten it under a rotating radar beam: a conic-gradient (one bright wedge fading to transparent along a decaying trail) used as a mask or overlay, its angle animated via a registered @property so it spins smoothly. Each letter lights as the beam passes and fades behind the trailing edge, like a sonar scanner sweeping the headline.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Cinema marquee bulb-chase frame
effects.technique: A padded rectangular frame around bold gold-gradient marquee lettering gets a background of evenly spaced repeating-radial-gradient warm bulb dots (each with its own soft glow ring), with background-position animated via steps() so every-other bulb lights in sequence - the classic theater chase. The word itself is a warm gold linear-gradient fill with a light bevel shadow.
effects.markupNote: single element with frame padding; optional ::before/::after strips for a fully closed rectangle of bulbs

effects.name: Blueprint technical schematic
effects.technique: Deep cyanotype-blue background panel behind hollow white-stroked letters (-webkit-text-stroke, transparent fill) so the glyphs read as line-art. Two repeating-linear-gradient layers (horizontal + vertical hairlines) form a faint graph-paper grid behind the text. ::before/::after draw short border segments as corner registration crosshairs, and a dashed border-bottom with small clip-path triangle arrowheads forms an annotated dimension line beneath the baseline. On light themes invert to navy ink on pale paper.
effects.markupNote: single element; pseudo-elements draw the grid/crosshairs/dimension line

effects.name: Nabla isometric palette cycle
effects.technique: Load Google Fonts' Nabla, a COLRv1 vector color font whose glyphs are isometric 3D blocks with built-in gradient shading. Declare two salted @font-palette-values (--pa/--pb) with override-colors, set font-palette on the scope, and animate/transition font-palette between them (Chromium interpolates gradient stops smoothly; other engines flip discretely). No background-clip - every color and the extrusion come from the font's own paint layers.
effects.markupNote: single element; requires loading font 'Nabla' (COLRv1 color font)
effects.browserSupport: all modern (static render); palette animation Chromium-only

effects.name: Foldit origami fold morph
effects.technique: Load Foldit, a COLRv1 + variable color font shaped like folded paper strips (Thin-Condensed to Extrabold-Expanded, with gradient shading that reads as dimensional creases). Set the family on the EffectDefinition and animate font-variation-settings 'wght'/'wdth' via @keyframes so the strips fold thin-to-bold and unfold, the font's embedded gradients selling the fold depth as it morphs.
effects.markupNote: single element; requires loading font 'Foldit' (COLRv1 variable color font)

effects.name: Honk truck-art shine morph
effects.technique: Load Honk (Ek Type's COLRv1 variable color font based on Indian truck lettering) with its MORF and SHLN axes and 8 built-in palettes. Animate font-variation-settings 'MORF' (ornament morph, simple-to-ornate) and 'SHLN' (shine line) via @keyframes so a bright shine sweeps through the glyphs while the decoration oscillates, and select a scheme via font-palette. Entirely font-native color and motion.
effects.markupNote: single element; requires loading font 'Honk' (COLRv1 variable color font)

effects.name: Rocher chromatic bevel
effects.technique: Load Rocher Color, a COLRv1 display font whose glyphs carry a glossy chromatic bevel built from four palette slots (ships 11 presets). Pick a base-palette and use @font-palette-values override-colors to retint the four slots to theme-appropriate hues. One static element renders a candy multi-color 3D-bevel wordmark with no CSS shadows at all.
effects.markupNote: single element; requires loading font 'Rocher Color' (COLRv1 color font, self-hosted - not on Google Fonts)

effects.name: Bungee chromatic layer stack
effects.technique: Load the Bungee family's purpose-built overprint layers - Bungee Regular + Bungee Inline + Bungee Shade. Stack three absolutely-positioned copies of the same word at identical size, each set to a different layer font and color: Shade behind as the cast block, Regular as the face, Inline as the light-groove highlight, producing dimensional multi-color signage. Optionally animate the Shade offset for a parallax lift.
effects.markupNote: stacked layer copies (3 absolutely-positioned spans of the same text); requires loading Bungee Regular + Inline + Shade

effects.name: Emphasis mark pop
effects.technique: Wrap one span per letter and apply text-emphasis (filled dot / sesame / triangle) with text-emphasis-position over, so a native mark sits above every glyph. Stagger each span's entrance (translateY + opacity) by index so letters and their marks pop up in sequence, and cycle text-emphasis-color through an animated @property hue. The dots are rendered by the emphasis engine, not extra DOM.
effects.markupNote: one span per letter

effects.name: Vertical stacked type
effects.technique: Set writing-mode:vertical-rl with text-orientation:upright on the scope so glyphs stack in an upright vertical column, a poster-spine headline; center the resulting tall box. Reveal via a clip-path/opacity wipe travelling down the column (or per-letter staggered drop-in). A genuine change of writing axis, not a transform rotating a horizontal line, so kerning and box flow stay correct.
effects.markupNote: single element (optional one span per letter for staggered drop-in)

effects.name: Swash flourish bloom
effects.technique: Load a face rich in OpenType swashes/stylistic sets (a swash serif or script). Resting state uses default glyphs; on :hover set font-feature-settings:'swsh' 1,'ss01' 1 (or font-variant-alternates:swash) so letters sprout ornamental tails and flourishes. Because glyph substitution is discrete, stack two copies (plain underneath, swash on top) and crossfade opacity on hover for a smooth bloom.
effects.markupNote: single element, optional 2 stacked copies for the crossfade; requires loading a swash OpenType font

effects.name: Illuminated drop cap
effects.technique: Style the scope's ::first-letter as an oversized illuminated capital: initial-letter:3 on Chromium/Safari (float + large font-size + line-height as the fallback), a gradient/ornamented fill via linear-gradient + -webkit-background-clip:text on ::first-letter, a thin contrast stroke and small soft drop-shadow, optional padding+box tint - while the rest of the word stays plain. A single-element manuscript versal; theme-aware accent, optional slow gradient shimmer on the cap only.
effects.markupNote: single element (uses the ::first-letter pseudo)
effects.browserSupport: all modern (initial-letter Chromium+Safari; float fallback elsewhere)

effects.name: Art deco gold-line lettering
effects.technique: Multi-stop gold linear-gradient clipped to the glyphs via background-clip:text. ::before/::after pseudo-elements draw a pair of thin flanking rules (border-top/border-bottom) capped with small rotated-square diamond ornaments, plus a low-opacity conic-gradient 'sunburst' of rays positioned behind the word. Wide letter-spacing + small-caps complete the poster-header geometry.
effects.markupNote: single element; ::before/::after draw flanking rules and sunburst

effects.name: Embroidery cross-stitch thread
effects.technique: Base glyph fill uses a tight repeating-linear-gradient at ~50deg with 2px light/dark stripes (background-clip:text) to simulate satin-stitch thread ridges. A second text layer is trimmed to a thin ring around each glyph via mask-composite:exclude (outer stroke mask minus inner), filled with a perpendicular dashed repeating-linear-gradient to render a running-stitch border. A faint repeating-conic-gradient checker sits behind at low opacity as fabric weave.
effects.markupNote: single element; one pseudo-layer for the running-stitch outline ring
effects.browserSupport: all modern (mask-composite needs a recent Firefox)

effects.name: Varsity chenille letterman patch
effects.technique: Bold rounded block letters filled with two overlaid fine repeating-radial-gradient dot layers at different angles/scales to fake a fuzzy chenille fiber texture, clipped to the text. Three nested rings (white, team-hue, black) are built from multi-directional hard text-shadow offsets at increasing radii to mimic a sewn patch border. A soft directional drop-shadow underneath sells the applique relief; an optional per-letter arc reuses the arc-text rotation pattern.
effects.markupNote: single element for the flat version; per-letter spans only if the arc toggle is enabled

effects.name: Windows-95 system bevel chrome
effects.technique: Flat mid-gray text fill topped with two stacked hard (0-blur) 1px text-shadows - light top-left, dark gray bottom-right - for a pixel-hard bevel. The scope element itself is padded and framed with the classic Win95 outset box-shadow trick (inset light edges top/left, inset dark edges bottom/right, plus a 1px solid outer border) so the whole word reads as an OS UI button/label chip. Monospace/system-font fallback, zero letter-spacing.
effects.markupNote: single element; box-shadow on the element itself forms the button chip

effects.name: Cyberpunk HUD targeting readout
effects.technique: Monospace cyan/green text with a modest text-shadow glow. ::before/::after each build an L-shaped corner bracket from two short border segments, positioned outside the text bounds on all four corners to form a targeting-reticle frame. A 1px bright gradient bar masked over the text and animated via background-position sweeps top-to-bottom like a radar scan. A row of variable-width repeating-linear-gradient dashes sits below the baseline as a data-readout tick strip.
effects.markupNote: single element; ::before/::after form the corner brackets, one extra mask layer drives the scan sweep

effects.name: Cassette futurism control panel label
effects.technique: A rounded 'label tape' chip in safety-orange or putty gets an inset box-shadow groove border to suggest recessed plastic, with four small radial-gradient corner dots standing in for rivets. The text itself is debossed via a light-above/dark-below text-shadow pair on a slightly darker fill, set in a wide-tracked monospace face, evoking a 1970s spacecraft control-panel placard.
effects.markupNote: single element; pseudo-elements or extra background layers place the four corner rivets

effects.name: Military crate safety stencil
effects.technique: Bold condensed lettering (Recursive pushed toward the MONO axis) filled flat in olive/black/safety-yellow. A mask-image built from two thin horizontal transparent bands across the whole text bounding box cuts literal stencil 'bridge gaps' through every glyph, mimicking a real cut stencil. The word sits inside a hazard rectangle built from a repeating-linear-gradient 45-degree yellow/black stripe border. Deliberately crisp and geometric rather than grungy.
effects.markupNote: single element; mask-image cuts the bridge gaps, background/border draws the hazard-stripe frame

effects.name: Airbrush pinstripe chrome badge
effects.technique: Bold italic slanted lettering filled with a chrome-red gradient whose edges are softened via filter:blur on a masked duplicate layer, simulating airbrush overspray feathering rather than a crisp gradient edge, topped with a bright specular chrome band. A second duplicate text layer offset a few px with a hard 0-blur text-shadow traces a second-color pinstripe line behind the slanted word, like a vinyl pinstripe decal, plus a soft ground shadow.
effects.markupNote: single element; ::before duplicates the text offset for the pinstripe line

effects.name: Neon selection styling
effects.technique: Style the scoped ::selection pseudo-element so dragging over the headline inverts it: color swaps to a bright hue, background-color flips to the text's resting color, and a layered text-shadow fires a neon bloom (text-shadow renders inside ::selection in Chromium/Safari). Resting state is a clean two-tone heading; the wow lands the instant the user selects the text.
effects.markupNote: single element

effects.name: Dock fisheye magnify
effects.technique: Per-letter spans; the letter under the cursor and its neighbors scale up by falloff exactly like the macOS dock, using text-length-independent combinators: .l:hover{scale:1.6}, .l:hover + .l and .l:has(+ .l:hover){scale:1.35}, plus distance-2 rules at scale:1.15. transform-origin bottom keeps the baseline; a transition on scale gives the elastic magnify. No JS, no pointer vars.
effects.markupNote: one span per letter

effects.name: Marquee ticker scroll
effects.technique: The headline is duplicated into two inline copies inside a width:max-content track set on an overflow:hidden scoped box; @keyframes translateX(-50%) loops the track seamlessly and infinitely. A mask-image linear-gradient fades both edges so glyphs dissolve in/out. Pauses on hover via animation-play-state for legibility.
effects.markupNote: text duplicated into two inline spans inside one track

effects.name: Spiral text path
effects.technique: Emit an inline <svg> with a <path> describing an Archimedean spiral (d sampled as r=a+b*theta into polar points) and a <textPath href> binding the headline to it, so letters wind inward toward the center over multiple loops. Salt the path id via svgId(). Static shape; fill/stroke recolor by theme.
effects.markupNote: inline svg: path + textPath

effects.name: Crop-mark frame
effects.technique: Use ::before and ::after (each with a couple of layered background linear-gradients or borders) to draw four corner crop-marks / registration brackets around the headline - the print-shop Ls at the box corners - without a continuous border. position:absolute to the four corners of the scoped box; optionally a subtle scaleX/opacity draw-in on load. Clean editorial/technical framing.
effects.markupNote: single element (two pseudo-elements)

effects.name: Follow underline glide
effects.technique: Per-letter spans, each with an ::after underline segment (scaleX:0 at rest). Hovering a letter grows only that letter's underline (.l:hover::after{scale:1}) while its immediate siblings get a partial reveal via .l:hover + .l::after and .l:has(+ .l:hover)::after, so the underline appears to slide toward and settle under whichever glyph the cursor is on. transition on transform gives the glide.
effects.markupNote: one span per letter
